continuity_tokens: same Japanese professional in early 30s across shots 01 to 03
same ivory or soft grey wardrobe family
brand_guardrails: do not make the frame look like a still life catalog shot
keep the reframe grounded and believable
no readable text, logos, labels, UI, or wordmarks anywhere in frame
the scene must fill the entire 9:16 canvas with no white bands, blank bars, inset-card framing, floating panel behavior, or smaller image area inside the frame
composition: hand, notebook, and glass of water as the core subjects
clean negative space for overlay copy inside the real scene
soft depth and simple tabletop geometry
the hand-and-reset action must be the true focal point, not the notebook
extend environment and tabletop logic naturally to all canvas edges
no blank white top or bottom padding and no letterbox-style empty zones
reference_strategy: Keep the frame lifestyle-real and text-safe, using the anchors only to control restraint, palette, and breathing room.
variation_guardrails: do not overcomplicate the desk styling
keep the shot calmer and more detailed than shot 01
preserve the same environment family and light direction
do not let the frame become a stock tabletop photo
reject and regenerate if any readable text or brand wordmark appears
reject and regenerate if the frame looks like an inset composition inside a taller canvas
reject and regenerate if top or bottom whitespace appears as blank bars instead of real in-scene breathing room

text_overlay.font_reference: Noto Sans JP or closest available sans-serif for Japanese

notes: Shot 02 reframe. Hard lock: absolutely no readable text or brand marks anywhere. Full-bleed 9:16 only, with real in-scene breathing room instead of white padding. This must read as a lived-in reset beat, not a stock tabletop photo.
